title: Root Layout (`app/layout.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the root layout for the entire application. It sets up the fundamental structure and global configurations that all other pages and components will inherit.

**Purpose:**

- **Global Structure:** Provides the basic HTML structure (`<html>`, `<body>`) for the application.
- **Font Configuration:** Integrates the Geist Sans and Geist Mono fonts, making them available globally via CSS variables.
- **Authentication Provider:** Wraps the entire application with `ClerkProvider` to enable authentication features.
- **Global Styling:** Applies global CSS classes for styling, including dark mode and antialiasing.

**Key Exports/Props:**

- **`metadata`:** An object conforming to Next.js's `Metadata` type. It defines the title and description for the application, which are used in the `<head>` section of the HTML.
- **`RootLayout` Component:** A React component that accepts `children` as a prop. This `children` prop represents the content of the currently rendered page or component, which gets injected into the layout.

**Usage Example:**

This `RootLayout` is automatically applied by Next.js to every page in the `app` directory. You don't need to explicitly import or use it within your pages. The layout's structure and global configurations will be present for all routes.

For instance, any page rendered within this layout will have access to the Geist fonts and the authentication context provided by `ClerkProvider`. The `className` applied to the `<html>` tag (`'--font-geist-sans' '--font-geist-mono' 'h-full' 'antialiased' 'dark'`) will be available for use in CSS modules or global stylesheets across the application.
